title: Kaadara
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{City
|image=[[File:Kaadara.png]]
|name=Kaadara
|constructed=c. [[2900 BBY]]<ref name="Founding">{{GalaxiesAED}} stated that Kaadara was founded about one [[Millennium/Legends|thousand]] [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the first [[Human/Legends|Human]] settlers arrived on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. According to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', the settlers arrived around [[3900 BBY/Legends|3900 BBY]]. This article's date is therefore derived from simple math based on this reference explanation.</ref>
|destroyed=
|rebuilt=
|builder=[[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo people]]<ref name="SWG:K">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=other}}Starting Location: "Kaadara"</ref>
|mayor=
|celestialbody=[[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]<ref name="SWG">{{GalaxiesAED}}</ref>

*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="SWG:P6">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=other}}Publish 6: The Imperial Crackdown</ref><ref name="Crackdown">{{SonyCite|url=content.jsp?page=Imperial%20Crackdown|text=Imperial Crackdown}}</ref>
}}
{{Quote|Good day citizen. Nowhere on Naboo is more beautiful than Kaadara this time of year, am I right? Of course I am.|Colonel Quarsh Panaka, to a citizen|<ref name="SYG:TTo">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Legacy|The Tip-off|Naboo}}</ref>}}
'''Kaadara''' was a small [[Ocean/Legends|oceanside]] [[City/Legends|city]] on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. Founded [[Millennium/Legends|thousands]] of [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] as a lavish retreat for [[Royal House of Naboo/Legends|Naboo royalty]], the city was unique among Naboo cities because the local populace welcomed interactions with the native [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]]. Under the city's first [[Regent/Legends|regent]], [[Kallos Sook]], the Gungans were allowed to serve as the city's [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|security force]]. Following Naboo's integration with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], Kaadara became a popular tourist destination, and this continued well into the height of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], when the city became a favorite of many [[Military rank/Legends|high-ranking]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moffs]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the Galactic Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], Kaadara was [[Garrison/Legends|garrisoned]] by Imperial forces in an effort to crack down on rebellious activity on Naboo. Meanwhile, the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Force]], having since supplanted the Gungan defense force, fought hard to defend the city from criminal elements, including those belonging to a gang of renegade [[Slicer/Legends|slicers]] known as the [[Skaak Tippers]]. Under the [[Monarch of Naboo/Legends|rule]] of [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Kylantha]], Kaadara also played host to the [[Festival of Love]], where it welcomed [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] from the [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] to share their rich culture with the [[Naboo (people)/Legends|people of Naboo]].

==Description==
Located along a scenic coastline in the northern reaches of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], Kaadara was one of the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] most luxurious tourist destinations. The small [[City/Legends|city]] was well known for its outdoor cafés, beautiful [[Beach/Legends|beaches]], and leisurely way of life.<ref name="SWG:K" /> By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], the city had grown to become a major center of commerce. The [[Architect/Legends|architecture]] of the city's buildings was reminiscent of those found throughout many of the world's other city's, such as [[Theed/Legends|Theed]].<ref name="SWG" />

The city consisted of the [[Kaadara Starport]], and many other venues and amenities, including a [[Kaadara cantina|cantina]], [[Kaadara theater|theater]], [[Kaadara hotel|hotel]], [[Kaadara medical center|medical center]] and [[Bank of Kaadara|bank]]. The southern boundary of the city saw the construction of an [[Imperial Post (Kaadara)|Imperial Post]],<ref name="SWG" /> established by the Empire during their efforts to crack down on the Rebellion.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|188|Kaadara}}</ref> The central area of Kaadara included a plaza with the city's hotel and four association halls. South of the plaza was the city's [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]], [[Bank/Legends|bank]] and [[Medcenter/Legends|medical center]]. Kaadara also had a small industrial area, next to which was a parking garage which offered [[Speeder/Legends|speeder]] repairs, and a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] port which offered travel service to other cities of Naboo.<ref name="SWG" />

==History==
===Founding===
[[File:KaadaraIndustrial.jpg|200px|thumb|A small industrial area of Kaadara]]
Around [[3900 BBY/Legends|3900 BBY]], a group of [[Human/Legends|Humans]] from the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core World]] of [[Grizmallt/Legends|Grizmallt]] colonized Naboo.<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> About a [[Millennium/Legends|thousand]] [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later,<ref name="SWG:K" /> around [[2900 BBY]],<ref name="Founding" /> the small city of Kaadara was founded along a scenic coastline in the northern reaches of the planet. The city was originally designed as a lavish retreat for [[Royal House of Naboo/Legends|Naboo royalty]] and their large entourages. Early in its history, Kaadara was unique among Naboo cities because the local populace welcomed, and even encouraged, interactions with the native [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]]. Kaadara's first [[Regent/Legends|regent]], [[Kallos Sook]], actually hired a large contingent of Gungans to serve as the city's [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|security force]]. For many [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]], these Gungan [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] lived on the outskirts of Kaadara, protecting the retreat and its visitors from wild [[Species/Legends|animals]] and outlaws.<ref name="SWG:K" />

===Republic era===
As Naboo gradually expanded its contact with other [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] worlds, Kadaara became a favorite destination for visiting dignitaries and wealthy travelers. Unfortunately for the populace, the increasing presence of outsiders led the suspicious Gungans to abandon their role as protectors of Kaadara. In the Gungans stead, the [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]] installed a small [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] of [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Force]] [[Officer/Legends|officers]] to patrol the city. These forces would be bolstered ten-fold whenever the current [[Monarch of Naboo/Legends|Monarch of Naboo]] arrived in Kaadara. Without the Gungans culling the local creatures, many native, but relatively harmless animal populations [[Reproduction/Legends|bred]] uncontrollably. This made the area around Kaadara a prime [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] ground for the pampered elite.<ref name="SWG:K" />

In [[54 BBY/Legends|54 BBY]],<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> the [[Vidar Kim's wife|wife]] and [[Vidar Kim's first son|two]] [[Vidar Kim's second son|sons]] of [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Vidar Kim]] were [[Death/Legends|killed]] in a tragic [[Starship/Legends|starship]] accident over the city.<ref name="Darth">[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']]</ref>

After the defeat of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]]<ref name="SWG:K" /> at the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="NEC" /> [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] encouraged the Kaadara regent to renew the city's long relationship with the Gungans. Many Gungans actually relocated to the city and, along with settlers from other worlds, formed the basis of Kaadara's powerful working class.<ref name="SWG:K" />

===Imperial era===
[[File:Barn_Sinkko.jpg|200px|thumb|Lieutenant Barn Sinkko inducted Imperial Navy pilots into the Imperial Inquisition during his time in Kaadara.]]
{{Quote|Take out as many of them as you can. Then find their leader, Jonni Skaak, and take him down as well. If you succeed, they won't be planning an attack on Kaadara again any time soon.|Colonel Quarsh Panaka, instructing a spacer to wipe out the Skaak Tippers|<ref name="SWG:Ttb">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Legacy|Tipping the balance|Naboo}}</ref>}}
Since the Galactic Empire's arrival on Naboo<ref name="SWG:K" /> in [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]],<ref name="Battlefront-II">''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]''</ref><ref name="Holocron22">{{Blog|holocron|22/comments|Movie Characters killed in the EU}}</ref> Kaadara's entrenched Gungan population was largely overlooked, perhaps because Imperial forces realized that the city, which was a favorite of many high-ranking [[Moff/Legends|Moffs]], would collapse without its able Gungan workforce.<ref name="SWG:K" /> During the Galactic Civil War, Rebel insurgents launched attacks on Kaadara in an attempt to occupy the city.<ref name="SWG:P6" /><ref name="Crackdown" /> In an effort to crack down on this kind of Rebel activity, Kaadara was garrisoned by Imperial forces.<ref name="CSWE" /> One [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]], a [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] named [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Darkstone]], considered retiring to a small place near Kaadara,<ref name="SWG" /> and [[Chief Inquisitor|Chief]] [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Loam Redge]] traveled to the city on at least one occasion.<ref name="Ruins">''[[Star Wars Galaxies: The Ruins of Dantooine]]''</ref> In fact, the city had become something of a base of operations for a special unit of Imperial Inquisition [[TIE pilot|pilots]] during this time. [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Barn Sinkko]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] was based there, and was responsible for inducting [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] into this secretive Inquisitorius [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]].<ref name="JTL">''[[Star Wars Galaxies: Jump to Lightspeed]]''</ref>

Kaadara, by now considered a major center of commerce, also became a hub for criminal elements. [[Smuggler/Legends|Smugglers]], mercenaries and [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] became a common sight, forcing those with a [[Bounty/Legends|price on their head]] to steer clear of the city's seedier areas.<ref name="SWG" /> A criminal organization led by [[Jonni Skaak]] known as the [[Skaak Tippers]] were also responsible for [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorizing]] the peaceful inhabitants of the city. [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Willoc]] of the RNSF went missing while investigating a Skaak Tipper plot to steal [[Bank/Legends|bank]] [[Computer/Legends|terminals]] from Kaadara, [[Slicer/Legends|hack]] into them, and gain access to the bank accounts of everyone in the city. However, their plans were [[Raid on the Skaak Tipper Hideout|foiled]] by [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Quarsh Panaka/Legends|Quarsh Panaka]],<ref name="SWG:Ttb" /> an Imperial loyalist<ref name="Complete">[[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']]</ref> who still oversaw activities in the RNSF by the time following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].<ref name="SWG:Ttb" />

Queen [[Kylantha]] meanwhile extended invitations to the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] to visit Naboo for annual celebrations of the [[Festival of Love]]. Despite being suspicious of outsiders, [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] [[Logray/Legends|Logray]] agreed to partake in the festivities along with [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]] and other representatives of the [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]]. Both Kaadara and the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of Theed played host to these festivities, allowing the the tribe the opportunity to share their rich culture with [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="SWG:EFoL">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=event|Ewok Festival of Love}}</ref><ref name="Love10">{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=68623&month=022010|text=Friday Feature – The Ewok Festival of Love}}</ref>

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|Coordinating the release of Imperial hostages has its challenges but also a high level of stress. After my retirement, I think I'll get a little place on Naboo near Kaadara.|Colonel Darkstone, to an Imperial soldier|<ref name="SWG:CD">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=other|Corellian Corvette Pre-Quest: "Colonel Darkstone"}}</ref>}}
The city of Kaadara was founded and inhabited by the people of Naboo. Early on, Kaadara was unique among the cities of Naboo because its populace welcomed the planet's native Gungans. Under the regency of Kallos Sook, these Gungans served as the city's early security force. While the importance of the Gungan population diminished following Naboo's integration with the Galactic Republic, they once again formed the basis of Kaadara's powerful working class under the rule of Queen Amidala.<ref name="SWG" />

During the height of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], Kaadara became a favorite of many high-ranking Imperial Moffs, and an appealing retirement destination. As a garrisoned Imperial city, the streets of Kaadara saw plentiful [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] patrols, including specialized [[stormrifle]]s, [[Stormtrooper sniper/Legends|stormsnipers]] and [[stormmedic]]s, among others, who were augmented by members of the Naboo police. [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transports]] also accompanied these Imperial street patrols. During times of Rebel occupation, the streets were overrun by various [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] of the Rebel Alliance, including [[Commando/Legends|commandos]] belonging to the [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|Alliance Special Forces]]. Otherwise, the city was nominally affiliated with the Galactic Empire, and served as a base of operations for many Imperial activities.<ref name="SWG" /> Lieutenant Barn Sinkko of the Imperial Navy was based out of one of the city's association halls,<ref name="JTL" /> while an undercover [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]] [[Agent/Legends|agent]] named [[Leb Slesher]] conducted business in the city's cantina. Slesher was served by yet another Human named [[Tamvar Senzen]] who stayed at the Kaadara hotel. Lieutenants [[Maximilian Vox]] and [[Harmon Vintollo]] were also stationed in the city, as was Colonel Panaka,<ref name="SWG:Ttb" /> an Imperial loyalist<ref name="Complete" /> who combated the Skaak Tippers in service of the Royal Naboo Security Force.<ref name="SWG:Ttb" /> Colonel Panaka thought highly of Kaadara, believing it to be one of the most beautiful places on Naboo.<ref name="SWG:SK">{{GalaxiesNGE}}</ref>

In addition to its sizeable staff of Imperial officers and other Human residents and visitors, Kaadara was inhabited by a number of Gungans, including [[Mullud Bombo]] and [[Huff Zinga]]. A [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] named [[Ketter Yaaran]] also frequented the city's cantina.<ref name="SWG" /> A [[Gotal/Legends|Gotal]] named [[Dakk]] also worked as an [[armorsmith]] in Kaadara for the [[Nal Raka Criminal Empire]].<ref name="SWG:FaA">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Finding an Armorsmith|Naboo}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[W:c:swg:Kaadara|Kaadara]] appeared as a city on Naboo in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'']], a [[Roleplaying game|massively multiplayer online role-playing game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref>

In an effort to improve the ''[[Star Wars]]'' "look and feel" of the game, the developers added a stronger Imperial presence to Kaadara as part of the "[[W:c:swg:Publish 6 Notes: 11 February 04|Imperial Crackdown]]" update,<ref name="Crackdown" /> released on [[February 11]], [[2004]].<ref name="P6TIC">{{SonyCite|url=recent_updates.jsp?o=60893|text=Publish 6: The Imperial Crackdown}}</ref> This entailed the implementation of a small Imperial base south of the city's starport. While Kaadara was officially recognized as an Imperial city both in the game<ref name="SWG:P6" /> and in other sources, such as the ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'',<ref name="CSWE" /> it was possible for the [[W:c:swg:Rebel Alliance|Rebel Alliance]] to occupy the city during the course of the Galactic Civil War's [[W:c:swg:Planetary control game|planetary control game]]. As a result, banners featuring the [[Alliance Starbird]] could appear throughout the city, and its streets could at times be patrolled by Rebel Alliance factional personnel.<ref name="SWG:P14">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=other}}Publish 14</ref>

In [[2008]], the [[W:c:swg:Ewok Festival of Love|Ewok Festival of Love]] was introduced to ''Star Wars Galaxies'' as a special event.<ref name="Love08">{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=67596&month=022008|text=Friday Feature – Celebrate the Love}}</ref> Starting in [[2009]], Kaadara was included as one of the cities which hosted the event,<ref name="Love09">{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=68214&month=022009|text=Friday Feature – So This Is Love}}</ref> and it continued to do so during subsequent Festival of Love events in [[2010]]<ref name="Love10" /> and 2011.<ref name="Love11">{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=69061&month=022011|text=Weekly Feature – Ewok Festival of Love Preview}}</ref>

The ''[[Star Wars Galaxies Trading Card Game]]'' expansion ''[[Squadrons Over Corellia]]'' included a card called "Tipping the Balance," the description of which mentioned Kaadara. However, the card description misspelled the city as "Kadaara."<ref name="SWGTCG">{{SWGTCG|set=Squadrons Over Corellia|cardname=Tipping the Balance}}</ref>
